qa_pairs.question: What is the difference in default user membership for cloud resources allocated via the Alliance's Rapid Access Service versus those allocated through the Resource Allocation Competition?
qa_pairs.answer: For cloud resources granted via the Rapid Access Service (RAS), all activated sponsored user roles are automatically members of the default Resource Allocation Project (RAP). In contrast, for cloud resources awarded through the Resource Allocation Competition (RAC RAP), only the Principal Investigator (PI) is a member by default, requiring sponsored users or other active Alliance users to be explicitly added to the RAC RAP.

qa_pairs.question: How can a Principal Investigator (PI) add sponsored users or co-PIs to a Cloud Resource Allocation Project (RAP) that was awarded through the Alliance's Resource Allocation Competition?
qa_pairs.answer: A PI can add members to a Cloud RAC RAP by logging into https://ccdb.alliancecan.ca/, navigating to "Manage RAP Memberships" under the "My Account" menu, selecting the specific RAP, and then either using the "In bulk" link under "Add Members" to include co-PIs and their associated sponsored user roles, or by entering a user's CCRI "One by one" if they are not available through the bulk option.

qa_pairs.question: What is the function of RAP membership as represented in LDAP for Alliance resources?
qa_pairs.answer: RAP membership, which is represented as a group in LDAP, defines a collection of users authorized to submit jobs against the Resource Allocation Project Identifier (RAPI) and share files within the same Unix group.
